title: Grouped CatBoost QueryCrossEntropy as a within-lap binary objective
summary: Previous research already pointed toward within-lap ranking.
rationale: Previous research already pointed toward within-lap ranking. CatBoost’s QueryCrossEntropy is a more specific next test for this task because it mixes ordinary logloss with a learned per-group shift, matching the idea that each race-lap has its own baseline pit propensity while still predicting binary next-lap events.
implementation_hint: Use GroupId = Year_Race_LapNumber and train CatBoost with QueryCrossEntropy over the same features, optionally with alpha sweeps and race-group sample weights. Blend its raw scores with the current binary CatBoost/XGB stack by rank or standardized logit rather than replacing the baseline outright.
expected_effect: Can improve ordering among drivers sharing the same race-lap context, which is exactly where many pit calls are marginal.
risk: If many groups have too few positives, the grouped objective may mostly relearn a noisy intercept; blend-first is safer than full replacement.
